 "cells": [

   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "16043fb4",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Walkthrough - SVMs and Kernels\n",
    "\n",
    "We will:\n",
    "1. Train a linear SVM and *see* the margin and the support vectors.\n",
    "2. Watch the soft-margin parameter $C$ trade margin width against violations.\n",
    "3. Use kernels to separate non-linearly separable data.\n",
    "4. Explore the $(C, \\gamma)$ landscape of the RBF kernel.\n",
    "\n",
    "Companion lesson: Lesson 01 - Support Vector Machines."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 1,
   "id": "a29edccd",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-09T08:18:20.922948Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-09T08:18:20.922457Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-09T08:18:22.843622Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-09T08:18:22.843077Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "from sklearn.datasets import make_blobs, make_moons\n",
    "from sklearn.model_selection import GridSearchCV, train_test_split\n",
    "from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler\n",
    "from sklearn.svm import SVC\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "def plot_svc_boundary(clf, X, y, ax, title=\"\"):\n",
    "    \"\"\"Decision boundary, margins (dashed), and support vectors (circled).\"\"\"\n",
    "    ax.scatter(X[:, 0], X[:, 1], c=y, cmap=\"coolwarm\", s=20)\n",
    "    xx, yy = np.meshgrid(\n",
    "        np.linspace(X[:, 0].min() - 0.5, X[:, 0].max() + 0.5, 300),\n",
    "        np.linspace(X[:, 1].min() - 0.5, X[:, 1].max() + 0.5, 300),\n",
    "    )\n",
    "    Z = clf.decision_function(np.c_[xx.ravel(), yy.ravel()]).reshape(xx.shape)\n",
    "    ax.contour(xx, yy, Z, levels=[-1, 0, 1], colors=\"k\", linestyles=[\"--\", \"-\", \"--\"], linewidths=1)\n",
    "    ax.contourf(xx, yy, Z, levels=[-np.inf, 0, np.inf], cmap=\"coolwarm\", alpha=0.15)\n",
    "    ax.scatter(*clf.support_vectors_.T, s=110, facecolors=\"none\", edgecolors=\"k\")\n",
    "    ax.set_title(title, fontsize=9)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "7c17bafa",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Part 1 - The maximum-margin hyperplane"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "a77a3c5e",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-09T08:18:22.845176Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-09T08:18:22.845054Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-09T08:18:23.503586Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-09T08:18:23.503155Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stderr",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-cfirhadar-Desktop-GitRepos-AAI/8a1435a8-b7fd-4fdf-b47f-a438a003816c/scratchpad/venv/lib/python3.10/site-packages/matplotlib/colors.py:2294: RuntimeWarning: invalid value encountered in subtract\n",
      "  resdat -= vmin\n",
      "/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-cfirhadar-Desktop-GitRepos-AAI/8a1435a8-b7fd-4fdf-b47f-a438a003816c/scratchpad/venv/lib/python3.10/site-packages/matplotlib/colors.py:2295: RuntimeWarning: invalid value encountered in divide\n",
      "  resdat /= (vmax - vmin)\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 600x500 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "n_support vectors: 3 of 60 points\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "X, y = make_blobs(n_samples=60, centers=2, cluster_std=1.0, random_state=6)\n",
    "\n",
    "clf = SVC(kernel=\"linear\", C=1e6).fit(X, y)  # huge C ~ hard margin\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 5))\n",
    "plot_svc_boundary(clf, X, y, ax, \"hard-ish margin: only the circled support vectors matter\")\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "print(f\"n_support vectors: {clf.n_support_.sum()} of {len(X)} points\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "6e651bb5",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "Delete any *non*-circled point and the solution would not move - the SVM summarizes\n",
    "the dataset by its boundary cases ($w=\\sum_i \\alpha_i y_i x_i$, $\\alpha_i > 0$ only on the margin).\n",
    "\n",
    "## Part 2 - The soft margin: sweeping C"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,

   "metadata": {

    }
   },

    {
     "name": "stderr",
     "output_type": "stream",

      "  resdat -= vmin\n",

      "  resdat -= vmin\n",
      "/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-cfirhadar-Desktop-GitRepos-AAI/8a1435a8-b7fd-4fdf-b47f-a438a003816c/scratchpad/venv/lib/python3.10/site-packages/matplotlib/colors.py:2295: RuntimeWarning: invalid value encountered in divide\n",

    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",

       "<Figure size 1500x400 with 3 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "X, y = make_blobs(n_samples=120, centers=2, cluster_std=1.9, random_state=6)  # overlapping classes\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(15, 4))\n",
    "for ax, C in zip(axes, [0.01, 1, 100]):\n",
    "    clf = SVC(kernel=\"linear\", C=C).fit(X, y)\n",
    "    plot_svc_boundary(clf, X, y, ax, f\"C = {C}: {clf.n_support_.sum()} support vectors\")\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "f3979145",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "Small $C$ -> wide, tolerant margin (many support vectors, more bias);\n",
    "large $C$ -> narrow margin chasing every point (fewer support vectors, more variance).\n",
    "\n",
    "## Part 3 - Kernels on non-separable data"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",

     "data": {

    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "X, y = make_moons(n_samples=200, noise=0.15, random_state=0)\n",
    "X = StandardScaler().fit_transform(X)\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(15, 4))\n",
    "for ax, (kernel, kw) in zip(\n",
    "    axes,\n",
    "    [(\"linear\", {}), (\"poly\", {\"degree\": 3}), (\"rbf\", {\"gamma\": 1.0})],\n",
    "):\n",
    "    clf = SVC(kernel=kernel, C=1.0, **kw).fit(X, y)\n",
    "    plot_svc_boundary(clf, X, y, ax, f\"{kernel} kernel, train acc = {clf.score(X, y):.2f}\")\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "ad5f06da",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "Same algorithm, different inner product: the RBF kernel separates the moons because a\n",
    "linear separator in its (infinite-dimensional) feature space is a nonlinear boundary here.\n",
    "\n",
    "### The $\\gamma$ knob is a length-scale"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,

   ],
   "source": [
    "fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(15, 4))\n",
    "for ax, gamma in zip(axes, [0.1, 1, 100]):\n",
    "    clf = SVC(kernel=\"rbf\", C=1.0, gamma=gamma).fit(X, y)\n",
    "    plot_svc_boundary(clf, X, y, ax, f\"RBF, gamma = {gamma}\")\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "bed6b877",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "`gamma=100` memorizes little islands around each training point - classic overfitting.\n",
    "\n",
    "## Part 4 - Tuning $(C, \\gamma)$ properly"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 6,
   "id": "a9fc60d3",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-09T08:18:25.186658Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-09T08:18:25.186546Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-09T08:18:27.561410Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-09T08:18:27.560968Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "best params: {'C': np.float64(100.0), 'gamma': np.float64(1.0)}, test accuracy: 1.000\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 600x400 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.4, random_state=0)\n",
    "\n",
    "param_grid = {\"C\": np.logspace(-2, 3, 6), \"gamma\": np.logspace(-2, 2, 5)}\n",
    "grid = GridSearchCV(SVC(kernel=\"rbf\"), param_grid, cv=5, n_jobs=-1).fit(X_train, y_train)\n",
    "print(f\"best params: {grid.best_params_}, test accuracy: {grid.score(X_test, y_test):.3f}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "scores = grid.cv_results_[\"mean_test_score\"].reshape(len(param_grid[\"C\"]), len(param_grid[\"gamma\"]))\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(6, 4))\n",
    "plt.imshow(scores, origin=\"lower\", cmap=\"viridis\")\n",
    "plt.colorbar(label=\"CV accuracy\")\n",
    "plt.xticks(range(len(param_grid[\"gamma\"])), [f\"{g:g}\" for g in param_grid[\"gamma\"]])\n",
    "plt.yticks(range(len(param_grid[\"C\"])), [f\"{c:g}\" for c in param_grid[\"C\"]])\n",
    "plt.xlabel(\"gamma\"); plt.ylabel(\"C\")\n",
    "plt.title(\"the good region is a diagonal band\")\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "0b93304f",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "### TODO exercises\n",
    "\n",
    "1. Refit the Part 2 models *without* `StandardScaler` on features multiplied by `[1000, 1]`.\n",
    "   What happens to the boundary, and why do kernels care about scaling when trees don't?\n",
    "2. Compare `SVC(kernel=\"linear\")` with `LogisticRegression` on the moons data - same template,\n",
    "   different loss (hinge vs log). Where do the boundaries differ?\n",
    "3. Compute the margin width $2/\\|w\\|$ for the three values of C in Part 2 (`clf.coef_`).\n",
    "4. For the best RBF model, plot `decision_function` values on the test set as a histogram,\n",
    "   split by class - these margins are what Platt scaling turns into probabilities."
   ]
  }
 ],
